childhood.familyBackground: Only son and second of three children of Howard Buffett, a congressman and stockbroker, and Leila Buffett. Raised in middle-class family embodying Midwestern simplicity and practicality. Father cultivated young Warren's business acumen and investing curiosity, exposing him to financial markets and entrepreneurship.
childhood.education: Entered Wharton School at University of Pennsylvania in 1947, then graduated from University of Nebraska-Lincoln at age 20. Went on to Columbia Business School where he studied under Benjamin Graham, adopting value investing philosophy. Returned to Omaha and taught 'Investment Principles' night class at University of Nebraska-Omaha.
childhood.earlyLife: Showed entrepreneurial spirit early: on first 1944 income tax return took $35 deduction for bicycle and watch use on paper route. In 1945 as high school sophomore, purchased used pinball machine with friend for $25, expanded to three machines in local barber shops by 1946. Sold business to war veteran in 1947 for $1,200. Father took him to visit New York Stock Exchange at age 10.
childhood.source: Wikipedia, Britannica Money, Vedantu, Clever Tykes
childhood.familyBackgroundKo: 주식 중개인이자 미국 하원 의원인 하워드 그레이엄 버켓트와 보험 중개인의 딸 레일라 스탤(후에 로일라라 불림)의 아들. 중산층 가정에서 자라며 금융과 사업에 대한 조기 노출. 형 로버트와 여동생 도리스가 있음.
childhood.educationKo: 오마하의 공립학교 졸업. 펜실베이니아 대학교와 네브래스카 대학교에서 학위 취득. 컬럼비아 대학교 비즈니스 스쿨에서 벤자민 그레이엄 교수로부터 가치 투자 이론 배움.
childhood.earlyLifeKo: 어린 나이부터 사업에 강한 관심을 보임. 6세 때 첫 사탕 판매, 11세 때 주식 거래 시작. 청소년 시절부터 신문 배달, 제핀스 가방 판매 등으로 수입 창출. 15세 때 벌써 6천 달러 이상의 자산을 축적.

careerTimeline.event: Graduated from Columbia Business School under Benjamin Graham. Worked as stockbroker and investment analyst while studying value investing philosophy.
careerTimeline.eventKo: 벤자민 그레이엄 밑에서 컬럼비아 경영대학원 졸업. 뉴욕 오퍼하이머 증권사에서 근무

careerTimeline.event: Took Dale Carnegie public speaking course, gaining confidence for teaching. Began teaching 'Investment Principles' at University of Nebraska-Omaha.
careerTimeline.eventKo: 데일 카네기 대중 연설 과정 수강, 평생의 교육 및 소통 능력에 자신감을 얻음

careerTimeline.event: Founded Buffett Partnership Ltd. in Omaha with initial capital of $105,100. Partnership achieved 30%+ compound annual returns from 1956-1969 while S&P averaged 7.4%.
careerTimeline.eventKo: 오마하에서 초기 자본 10만 5천 달러로 버핏 파트너십 설립

careerTimeline.event: Became a millionaire through the success of his investment partnerships, which had grown to 11 entities managing nearly $7.2 million.
careerTimeline.eventKo: 투자 파트너십의 성공으로 백만장자가 됨; 합산 수익률 항상 시장 평균 초과

careerTimeline.event: Began acquiring Berkshire Hathaway stock for his partnership at $7.50 per share. Fund owned 7% of company within a year.
careerTimeline.eventKo: 파트너십을 통해 주당 7.50달러에 버크셔 해서웨이 주식 매입 시작

careerTimeline.event: Gained control of Berkshire Hathaway, a struggling textile manufacturer. Began transforming it into a diversified holding company.
careerTimeline.eventKo: 고전하는 섬유 제조업체 버크셔 해서웨이의 경영권 획득; 보험 사업으로의 전환 시작

careerTimeline.event: Emerged as Berkshire Hathaway's chairman and majority shareholder. Transformed the company from textile manufacturing into an investment and insurance conglomerate.
careerTimeline.eventKo: 버크셔 해서웨이의 회장 겸 대주주로 부상. 섬유에서 보험 및 투자 지주회사로 전환

careerTimeline.event: Charlie Munger joined Berkshire Hathaway as vice-chairman and long-time business partner. Munger's influence shaped Berkshire's investment strategy and culture.
careerTimeline.eventKo: 찰리 멍거가 버크셔 해서웨이의 부회장이자 오랜 투자 파트너로 합류

careerTimeline.event: Berkshire Hathaway acquired Nebraska Furniture Mart for $60 million, exemplifying Buffett's preference for high-quality businesses with strong competitive advantages.
careerTimeline.eventKo: 버크셔 해서웨이가 네브래스카 퍼니처 마트를 6천만 달러에 인수, 소매 투자 전략 확대

careerTimeline.event: Began major investment in The Coca-Cola Company, eventually purchasing up to 7% of the company for $1.02 billion. Largest single stock investment of Berkshire Hathaway.
careerTimeline.eventKo: 코카콜라 회사에 대규모 투자 시작, 최종적으로 주식의 7%를 인수하여 투자 역사상 가장 상징적인 거래 중 하나로 기록

careerTimeline.event: During financial crisis, made strategic investments while market collapsed. Berkshire positioned as safe haven during turmoil.
careerTimeline.eventKo: 금융 위기 동안 시장 폭락 속에서 전략적 투자 실행; 골드만삭스에 50억 달러 우선주 투자

careerTimeline.event: Berkshire Hathaway acquired insurance company Alleghany Corporation for $11.6 billion in largest acquisition in Buffett era.
careerTimeline.eventKo: 버크셔 해서웨이가 보험사 앨러게니 코퍼레이션을 116억 달러에 인수

careerTimeline.event: Announced Greg Abel as successor CEO, effective January 1, 2026. Buffett remained as chairman at age 95.
careerTimeline.eventKo: 2026년 1월 1일부터 그렉 아벨을 후임 CEO로 발표. 버핏은 92세에 이사회 의장으로 남음

careerTimeline.event: Greg Abel assumed role of President and CEO on January 1, 2026, with Buffett continuing as chairman.
careerTimeline.eventKo: 그렉 아벨이 2026년 1월 1일 사장 겸 CEO 취임, 버핏은 이사회 의장으로 남아 전략 자문 역할 수행

failures.description: Berkshire Hathaway textile business was struggling when Buffett took control. The company was a failing textile manufacturer with declining competitiveness and poor economics.
failures.lesson: Rather than try to fix the textile business, Buffett learned to pivot and transform the company into a holding company and investment vehicle. He has called Berkshire Hathaway 'the dumbest stock I ever bought' in 2010, but the name was retained as a constant reminder of the costly mistake and lessons learned. This failure taught him the importance of reinventing failing businesses.
failures.descriptionKo: 솔로몬 브라더스 채권 스캔들(1991). 투자한 회사의 트레이더들이 미국 재무부 채권 거래 규칙을 위반. 회사의 신뢰성이 크게 손상되고 막대한 비용 발생.
failures.lessonKo: 버켓은 투자한 회사의 인적 자산과 경영진의 윤리성이 얼마나 중요한지를 배움. 수익성 있는 사업도 도덕적 문제가 있으면 장기적으로 가치를 잃는다는 것을 깨달음.

failures.description: Invested heavily in ConocoPhillips (U.S. oil major) in 2008 just before oil prices peaked during the financial crisis. Betting on sustained high oil prices, Buffett suffered significant losses when oil collapsed during the credit meltdown.
failures.lesson: Buffett admitted to 'terrible timing' and learned harsh lessons about market cycles and the risks of sector concentration. This investment mistake reinforced the importance of avoiding timing the market and recognizing cyclical industry risks.
failures.descriptionKo: 제너럴 리 운영 케이블 TV 회사 인수(1986). 케이블 산업의 전망을 잘못 평가하여 투자 손실 발생. 비용도 증가하고 수익도 기대에 미치지 못함.
failures.lessonKo: 버켓은 자신이 완전히 이해하지 못하는 산업에는 투자하지 않아야 한다는 가치 투자의 핵심 원칙을 다시 확인함.

failures.description: Tesco investment in 2011 resulted in a $444 million after-tax loss. Buffett held the position too long, failing to exit when warning signs emerged about the U.K. supermarket chain's operational challenges.
failures.lesson: Buffett publicly stated 'An attentive investor, I'm embarrassed to report, would have sold Tesco shares earlier.' This humbled admission reinforced lessons about the importance of timely exit strategies and recognizing when investment theses have broken down.
failures.descriptionKo: 텍사스 유틸리티 사(1996). 전력 산업의 구조 변화를 제대로 예측하지 못하여 투자 수익이 저조. 규제 환경의 변화로 예상 수익이 현실화되지 않음.
failures.lessonKo: 산업의 구조적 변화와 규제 환경의 변화를 신중히 분석해야 한다는 교훈. 과거의 성공 패턴이 미래에도 적용된다고 가정해서는 안 된다는 것을 배움.

quotes.text: Be fearful when others are greedy, and greedy when others are fearful.
quotes.context: Buffett's most widely cited quote encapsulating his core contrarian investing philosophy and emotional discipline. Encourages buying when prices are depressed and others are pessimistic.
quotes.textKo: 가치 있는 것을 저렴하게 사라. 이것이 성공의 비결이다.
quotes.contextKo: 버켓의 기본적인 투자 철학과 가치 투자의 핵심 원칙

quotes.text: Rule No. 1: Never lose money. Rule No. 2: Never forget Rule No. 1.
quotes.context: Buffett's fundamental rules summarizing his investing philosophy centered on capital preservation and risk management as the foundation of wealth creation.
quotes.textKo: 위험은 당신이 무엇을 하고 있는지 알지 못할 때 온다.
quotes.contextKo: 투자 전에 철저한 분석과 이해의 중요성에 관해

quotes.text: Time is the friend of the wonderful company, the enemy of the mediocre.
quotes.context: Emphasizing the power of compounding over long periods and the importance of investing in high-quality businesses with sustainable competitive advantages.
quotes.textKo: 성공의 첫 번째 규칙은 손해 보지 않는 것이고, 두 번째 규칙은 첫 번째 규칙을 잊지 않는 것이다.
quotes.contextKo: 자본 보전의 중요성과 장기 투자 전략의 기초

quotes.text: It's far better to buy a wonderful company at a fair price, than a fair company at a wonderful price.
quotes.context: Buffett articulating his value investing approach: quality matters more than price alone. Encourages investors to focus on business quality and durability of competitive advantage.
quotes.contextKo: 능력 범위 내에서의 투자와 철저한 사전 조사의 필요성

quotes.text: Beware the investment activity that produces applause; the great moves are usually treated by yawns.
quotes.context: Warning against following crowd psychology or trending investments. True investment wisdom often looks boring and unconventional to the market.
quotes.textKo: 시간은 훌륭한 회사와 나쁜 회사의 친구이자 적이다.
quotes.contextKo: 장기 투자의 복리 효과와 열악한 기업 구조의 악화에 관해

authored.title: The Essays of Warren Buffett: Lessons for Corporate America
authored.note: Collection of Buffett's written essays on investing principles, capital allocation, and corporate governance.
authored.noteKo: 버켓이 연간 주주 편지는 투자와 기업 경영에 관한 고전으로 여겨짐

authored.title: Berkshire Hathaway Letters to Shareholders (1965-2024)
authored.note: Annual shareholder letters spanning 60 years, providing insights into Berkshire's strategy, investments, and Buffett's investment philosophy.
authored.noteKo: 기업 지배 구조와 경영 윤리에 관한 저작

authored.title: Buffett Partnership Letters (1956-1970)
authored.note: Compilation of letters to partners during early years of Buffett Partnership Limited, revealing the development of his investing strategy.
authored.noteKo: 금융 시장과 투자 전략에 관한 분석

recommended.title: The Intelligent Investor
recommended.author: Benjamin Graham
recommended.why: Buffett's most frequently recommended book, picked up at age 19. He calls it 'the best book on investing ever written.' Graham's value investing philosophy fundamentally shaped Buffett's approach.
recommended.whyKo: 벤자민 그레이엄이 저술한 가치 투자의 기본서. 버켓이 평생 추종하는 투자 철학의 근간

recommended.title: Poor Charlie's Almanack
recommended.author: Charlie Munger
recommended.note: Collection of speeches and essays capturing Munger's approach to decision-making through mental models from multiple disciplines. Praised at nearly every Berkshire annual meeting.
recommended.whyKo: 버켓이 추천하는 경제학 및 금융 책. 시장 심리와 합리적 의사결정의 중요성을 다룸

recommended.title: The Outsiders
recommended.author: William N. Thorndike Jr.
recommended.why: Outstanding book about CEOs who excelled at capital allocation, profiling eight unconventional executives whose companies dramatically outperformed the market. Includes discussion of Berkshire Hathaway.
recommended.whyKo: 기업 경영과 리더십에 관한 실용적 조언. 버켓이 경영 철학으로 삼는 책

recommended.title: Shoe Dog
recommended.author: Phil Knight
recommended.why: Buffett called this 'the best book I read last year' in his 2016 letter. Memoir of Nike's founder appealing to Buffett's interest in entrepreneurial journeys and business building.
recommended.whyKo: 재무 분석과 기업 평가에 관한 고전

personalTraits.knownFor: The 'Oracle of Omaha,' legendary value investor and capital allocator. Known for exceptional business judgment, patience, emotional discipline, and ability to identify undervalued companies with durable competitive advantages. Simplicity and frugality despite enormous wealth—still lives in his modest Omaha home purchased in 1958. Shareholder-friendly approach to capital allocation at Berkshire Hathaway. Known for wisdom, humility, witty shareholder letters, and mentoring. One of the greatest wealth creators in history through compound returns.
personalTraits.philanthropy: Signed The Giving Pledge in 2010, committing to donate more than 99% of wealth to philanthropy during lifetime or upon death. Donated over $60 billion through 2025, primarily to Gates Foundation focusing on global health, poverty reduction, and disease eradication. Revised philanthropic plans in 2024-2025, determining original plans 'not feasible.' Now leaving three children with $500 million per year each to distribute through family charitable foundations, creating 'largest experiment in family giving of our time.' Emphasized importance of Rule of Law in philanthropic work.
personalTraits.controversies: Relationship with Gates Foundation became strained after Bill Gates' 2021 divorce from Melinda. Buffett had concerns about the foundation becoming 'bureaucratically bloated.' Resigned from Gates Foundation board in 2022 after Bill and Melinda's separation. Revised giving plans have drawn criticism from some who believe distribution through family foundations preserves donor control over philanthropy. Some analysis suggests Giving Pledge signatories have not matched stated commitments—approximately 80% of Buffett's contributions have gone to intermediary organizations rather than direct operating charities. Criticized by some for accumulating vast wealth while global poverty persists.
personalTraits.knownForKo: 세계 최고의 투자가로 인정받는 기업인. 벌크셔 해서웨이를 작은 방직 회사에서 거대한 투자 지주사로 변모시킴. 50년 이상 연평균 20% 이상의 수익률 달성. 단순하고 절제된 생활 방식으로 유명. 가치 투자의 대가이자 장기적 관점의 사업가. 장기적인 사업 관점과 합리적 사고로 칭송받음.
personalTraits.philanthropyKo: 빌 게이츠와의 오랜 우정으로 글로벌 보건과 교육에 관심을 가짐. 2006년 게이츠 재단에 기부를 시작하여 최종적으로 수십억 달러를 기부할 것으로 약속. 현재 벌크셔 해서웨이의 자산을 자선 목적으로 사용할 계획. 미국 내 빈곤 퇴치와 교육 개선에도 관심을 보임.
personalTraits.controversiesKo: 비교적 논쟁이 적으나 일부 주제: 버켓이 투자한 회사들의 일부가 환경 문제를 야기한다는 비판; 부의 집중과 불평등 문제에 관한 논쟁; 투자 스타일의 변화와 관련하여 과거 성공이 반복되지 않을 수 있다는 우려; 버켓의 고령 나이와 경영 계획 이행 능력에 관한 우려.

sajuConnection.note: This field will be written by us. Leave null.
